title: Pendal

'''Pendal''' was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="AIR" /> located in the [[Pendal system/Legends|Pendal system]], a part of the [[Homon sector/Legends|Homon sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the site of the [[City/Legends|settlement]] known as [[Tilam City]], and technology that was relatively simpler than high-technology tools available in the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] was in common use on the celestial body.<ref name="AIR" />

The [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Callandri]] grew up in Tilam City, being involved in crime of all varieties since a very [[Youngling/Legends|young age]]. While employed by various criminal organizations on Pendal, she developed [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] skills.<ref name="AIR" /> At some point by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', which establishes that [[Callandri]] had traveled off Pendal at least several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to its setting, represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one year before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Callandri traveled offworld, eventually becoming an assassin who used relatively simple technology such as a [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrower]] [[submachinegun]] and a [[Q-6100]] [[Landspeeder/Legends|groundspeeder]].<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Pendal was mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Pendal [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square M-7.<ref name="TEA" />
